Alif Lām Ra. Those are the signsFootnote-1of the Scripture and an evident RecitalFootnote-2.
Perhaps those who have denied will wish they had been Muslims.
LetFootnote-3them eat and enjoy and be distracted by(false) hope, for they willcome to know.
For in no way did We annihilate any town except that it had a known written decree.
No community can advance its time, nor can they delay it.
And they said, “O you upon whom the Reminder has been bestowed from on high, very truly you are insane.
If only you would bring us the angels, if you are one of the truthful!”
We bestowFootnote-4 not the angelsfrom on high except with the truthFootnote-5, and in no way would theyFootnote-6then be respited.
Indeed, We—We alone—have bestowed the Reminder from on high, and We will most surely preserve it.
And most surely We sent(messengers) before youFootnote-7to the sects of the ancients,
but no messenger comesFootnote-8to them but they used to ridicule him.
Thus We insert itFootnote-9into the hearts of the criminals—
they do not believe in it, though the rule of(God regarding) the ancients has surely passed before.
And if We were to open for them a gateway into the sky so that they were to continue ascending through it,
they would surely say, “Indeed, our sight(s) have been stupefiedFootnote-10 ; rather, we are a people bewitched.”
And most surely, We have made constellations in the sky and adorned it to the beholders;
and We have preserved it from every outcast devil,
except the one who eavesdrops and is followed by an evident meteor.
And the land—We spread it and cast firm mountains in it, and We germinated therein of everything in proper measure.
And We made livelihoods for youFootnote-11in it, as well as for those for whom you are not the providers.
For there is not a thing but with Us are its treasuries, yet in no way do We bestow it from on high except in a known measure.
And We sent the windsFootnote-12 , fertilizing, then sent down water from the sky and gave it to youFootnote-13to drink, and in no way are you its keepers.
And indeed, We—surely, We—give life and cause death, and We are the Inheritors.
And most surely We have known those of you who passed away, and most surely We have known those who come afterwards;
and indeed, it is yourFootnote-14Lord Who herds themFootnote-15together; indeed, He is All-Wise, All-Knowing.
And most surely We created man from potter’s clay, of fermented mud,
while We created the unseen beingFootnote-16before from the fire of the searing wind.
And recall when yourFootnote-17Lord said to the angels, “I am creating a human being from potter’s clay, of fermented mud.
So when I have formed him and breathed into him of My spirit, fall down to him, prostrating.”
So the angels prostrated themselves, all of them together—
but not IblisFootnote-18. He refused to be with those who prostrated themselves.
He said, “O IblisFootnote-19, what keeps you from being with those who prostrate themselves?”
He said, “Never would I prostrate myself to a human being whom You created from potter’s clay, of fermented mud.”
He said, “Then get out of itFootnote-20, for indeed you are an outcast,
and indeed the curse is upon you until the Day of Recompense.”
He said, “My Lord, then reprieve me until the day they are resurrected.”
He said, “Then, you are indeed of the ones reprieved
until the Day of the Time Appointed.”
He said, “My Lord, since You have deluded me, I will most surely adorn for them(evil deeds) on earth, and I will most surely delude them all,
except for Your sincere chosenFootnote-21 servants among them.”
He said, “This is a straight path which is binding on MeFootnote-22 :
over My servants youFootnote-23have no authority indeed, except for those who follow you of the deluded ones.
And indeed, Hell is the meeting place for them all.
It has seven gates; for each gate is an assigned class.”
Indeed, the mindful(of God) are amid Gardens and springs.
“Enter it in peace, feeling safe.”
And We removedFootnote-24whatever malice was within their chests—as brethren, on couches facing each other.
No fatigue touchesFootnote-25them therein, nor from it will they ever be brought out.
InformFootnote-26My servants that I—only I—am the Oft-Forgiving, the Bestower of mercy,
and that My punishment is the painful punishment.
And inform them of the guests of Abraham,
when they entered upon him and said, “Peace.” He said, “We are truly scared of you.”
They said, “Do not be scared; we bring youFootnote-27glad tidings of a knowledgeable boy.”
He said, “Have you brought me glad tidings when old age has touched me? So what glad tidings could you giveFootnote-28 ?”
They said, “We brought you true glad tidings, so be not of the despairing ones.”
He said, “And who despairs of his Lord’s mercy except for thoseFootnote-29astray?”
He said, “So what is your business, O emissaries?”
They said, “We have been sent to a people of criminals,
except for Lot’s folk; we will most surely deliverFootnote-30 them all,
except for his wife.” We have determined that she will most surely be of those who linger behind.
So when the emissaries came to Lot’s folk,
he said, “You are an unknown people.”
They said, “Rather, we have come to youFootnote-31with that about which they were skeptical,
and we have brought you the truth, and very truly we are truthful.
So journey with your family at the latter part of the night and follow at their rear, and let none of you turn around, and proceedFootnote-32wherever youFootnote-33are commanded.”
And We decreed to him that matter: that the last trace of these would be uprooted by the morning.
And the people of the town came anticipating glad tidings.
He said, “Indeed, these are my guests, so do not embarrass me,
and be mindful of Allah and do not disgrace me.”
They said, “Did we not forbid youFootnote-34from(receiving) any peopleFootnote-35?”
He said, “These are my daughters, if you must.”
By yourFootnote-36life, in their drunkenness they certainly do blunder!
So the Shout took them away at sunrise.
And We turned it upside down and rained down upon them stones of hard clay.
Indeed, in that are certainly signs for those who contemplate.
And indeed, itFootnote-37is certainly on an existing way.
Indeed, in that is certainly a sign for the believers.
And indeed, the fellows of the Thicket were certainly unjust,
so We took retribution on them, and indeed, both are certainly in an evident archive.
And most surely, the fellows of Al-HijrFootnote-38disbelievedthe emissaries.
So We brought them Our signs, but they used to disregard them.
And of the mountains they used to carve homes, feeling safe.
But the Shout took them away by the morning,
so all what they used to earn was of no avail to them.
And We did not create the heavens and the earth and whatever lies between them except with the truthFootnote-39. And indeed, the Hour is coming, so pardonFootnote-40(with) gracious pardoning.
Indeed, yourFootnote-41Lord is the One Who is the All-Creating, the All-Knowing.
And most surely, We have brought youFootnote-42seven of the oft-repeatedFootnote-43and the great RecitalFootnote-44.
DoFootnote-45not look longingly at the good things We have given some classes of them to enjoy, and do not grieve over themFootnote-46, and lower your wingFootnote-47towards the believers.
And say, “Indeed, it is I—I who am the evident warner.”
So too did We send down to those who divide among themselves—
those who have made the RecitalFootnote-48into fragments.
So by yourFootnote-49Lord, We will surely ask them all
about what they used to do.
So proclaimFootnote-50openly what you are commanded and disregard the polytheists.
Indeed, We have sufficed youFootnote-51against the mockers:
those who set up another god with Allah. So they will come to know.
And most surely We are aware that yourFootnote-52chest tightens with what they say.
So exalt your Lord with praise and be of those who prostrate,
and worship your Lord until CertitudeFootnote-53comes to you.
